title: Hash Tables, Dictionaries, Sets, and Collision Policy · Invariant Lens: Hash Contract
summary: Create a deduplication and lookup service that remains correct under collision-heavy data. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa hash contract increment in a hash-table model and workload benchmark. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.
Test underflow, overflow, wraparound, duplicates, and long operation sequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dsa-hash-contract — Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.
class CollisionKey:
    def __init__(self, value: str): self.value = value
    def __hash__(self): return 2
    def __eq__(self, other): return isinstance(other, CollisionKey) and self.value == other.value

index = {CollisionKey("aa"): 1, CollisionKey("bb"): 2}
assert len(index) == 2
